findings.description: Form input boxes are missing from the table cells.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, Columns A and B contain rectangular boxes for user input followed by '.00'. In the HTML, the visual input boxes are entirely missing, and the cells only contain the static text '.00'.

findings.description: Form input boxes are missing from the table cells.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, Columns A and B contain rectangular boxes for user input followed by '.00'. In the HTML, the visual input boxes are entirely missing, and the cells only contain the static text '.00'.

PDF headers are 'COLUMN A Amount from Form D-400 Schedule S' and 'COLUMN B Amount of Column A Attributable to N.C.'. HTML headers are shortened to 'from Form D-400 Schedule S' and 'Column A Attributable to N.C.'.

findings.description: Loss of visual indentation for sub-items in the table.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, items '27a. N.C. Sourced' and '27b. Non-N.C. Sourced' are visually indented under item 27 to show hierarchy. In the HTML, they are aligned flush left with all other items.

findings.description: Textual content from the Page 1 infographic is missing from the HTML and only represented by an image.
findings.evidence: The PDF's first page contains explicit textual instructions such as 'Do not use red ink. Use blue or black ink.', 'Set page scaling to "none."', and 'Do not submit photocopies of returns. Submit originals only.' The HTML completely omits this text, representing the entire page with a single image tag: ``.
